subject: PhD Topology
instructions: Answer questions 1–4 without giving proofs. Answer questions 5–10, giving proofs or counterexamples.

subparts.label: a)
subparts.text: What does it mean for \(X\) to be a retract of \(Y\)?

subparts.label: b)
subparts.text: What does it mean for \(X\) to be a deformation retract of \(Y\)?

problems.text: Let \(K\) be a finite simplicial complex. Define \(\chi(K)\), the Euler characteristic of \(K\).
Define what it means for a topological space to be
State the Brouwer fixed point theorem.
Let \(p:X \to Y\) be a \(d\)-sheeted covering projection, where \(X\) and \(Y\) are finite simplicial complexes. Show that \(\chi(X)=d\cdot\chi(Y)\).
Compute the fundamental group of a closed, orientable surface \(X\) of genus 3.
Let \(X\) be a closed, orientable surface of genus 3.

subparts.label: b)
subparts.text: Compute the Euler characteristic of this surface \(X\).

problems.text: Show that every continuous map \(f:S^2 \to S^1\) is homotopic to a constant map.
For \(i=1,2,\ldots\), let \(P_i\) be a 2-dimensional plane in \(\mathbb{R}^3\).
Let \(C\) be the Cantor set and let \(S\) be any compact metric space. Show that there is a continuous map \(f:C \to S\) such that \(f\) is onto.
